title: Coelogyne merapiensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne merapiensis is a species of orchid native to Indonesia. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Coelogyne is a large and diverse group of orchids, with over 200 species. Coelogyne merapiensis is further classified as part of the tribe Arethuseae. This classification helps to organize and understand the relationships between different orchid species.

intro: Coelogyne merapiensis, also known as Merapi Coelogyne or Java Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to Indonesia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne merapiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelogyne merapiensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to make teas and tinctures that are believed to promote health and well-being.
